verdict.label: Go bodyboarding
verdict.headline: Go bodyboarding: Supertubos Lunch
verdict.reason: Supertubos looks like the best bodyboard option for lunch.  This should feel like a fun progression session rather than a heavy one.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is lining up for a powerful wedge, so keep the comfort level in mind.

best.bestWind: ideal N/NNE/NE, usable NNW/ENE, clean <=22 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Supertubos looks like a fun-sized window for Worth checking, High confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 145 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 47/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort; 10-13s at mid tide is the prime barrel / wedge setup for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.  Expect: respect the power, rips and crowd because it turns heavy fast.
best.shortExplanation: Supertubos is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is lining up for a powerful wedge, so keep the comfort level in mind.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Medium confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 295 kJ, 11 s period, consistent 66/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push; 10-13s at mid tide suits the reef shelves and gives long workable walls.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should help the reef hold shape.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a solid window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 1.2-1.5 m, 346 kJ, 12 s period, consistent 73/100, and wind is the main risk.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 10-13s at mid tide works well on exposed beaches.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a solid window for Heavy / advanced, Medium confidence: 1.2-1.8 m, 499 kJ, 12 s period, workable consistency 57/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 10-13s at low tide can still work well on Belgas; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; Rey Cortiço is above your current comfort range; gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy.
ranked.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (18:00 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
Rey Cortiço is above your current comfort range
gusts over 35 km/h could make it messy
